data.name: Settings_GMS14Path_Tooltip
data.value: Required only if you want to use the Studio runner rather than the .exe or run the game under debugger.

data.name: Settings_GMS2RuntimesPath_Tooltip
data.value: Required only if you want to run GMS2 games using the Studio runner rather than the .exe

data.name: Settings_AssetOrderSwapping_Tooltip
data.value: Toggles dragging & dropping assets in the asset tabs to different positions in the list. Disabled by default.

data.name: Settings_AutoFileAssociation_Tooltip
data.value: Automatic file association. Enabled by default.

data.name: Settings_ShowDebuggerOption
data.value: Show "Run game under GMS debugger" file option

data.name: Settings_ShowDebuggerOption_Tooltip
data.value: Whether to show the option in the "File" menu. Disabled by default.

data.name: Settings_RememberWindowPlacements
data.value: Remember last window position and size

data.name: Settings_RememberWindowPlacements_Tooltip
data.value: Whether to restore window position and size when launching a new tool instance. Disabled by default.

data.name: Settings_WarnOnClose_Tooltip
data.value: Warn about saving before closing. Enabled by default.

data.name: Settings_TempRunMessage_Tooltip
data.value: Warn about temp running. Enabled by default.

data.name: Settings_ShowNullEntries
data.value: Show null entries in resource tree

data.name: Settings_ShowNullEntries_Tooltip
data.value: Whether to show null entries in the resource tree, which in GM 2024.11+ games usually were unused assets removed by the compiler. Disabled by default due to limited support in the GUI.

data.name: Settings_RecompileAllCodeSources
data.value: Recompile all source GML when saving a project data file

data.name: Settings_RecompileAllCodeSources_Tooltip
data.value: Whether to recompile all GML source code included in the currently-loaded project, if one is loaded, whenever saving a data file (including temp runs). Disabled by default.

data.name: Settings_EnableDarkMode_Tooltip
data.value: Makes the program interface dark. Disabled by default.

data.name: Settings_GlobalGridWidth_Tooltip
data.value: This option globally overrides the automatic assignment of a room's grid width based on the most used tile's width in that room.

data.name: Settings_GlobalGridHeight_Tooltip
data.value: This option globally overrides the automatic assignment of a room's grid height based on the most used tile's height in that room.

data.name: Settings_GlobalGridThickness_Tooltip
data.value: This option globally overrides the automatic assignment of a room's grid thickness.

data.name: GML_GeneralSettings_Tooltip
data.value: Settings that apply to both the compiler and decompiler, to make editing GML code simpler.

data.name: GML_UseCSSHexColors_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, color constants can be formatted in the #RRGGBB hex format.

data.name: GML_InstanceIdPrefix_Tooltip
data.value: Prefix used for named instance IDs when found by decompiler, and for compilation. Change this value if it conflicts with variable names.

data.name: GML_DecompilerSettings_Tooltip
data.value: Settings that solely apply to the GML decompiler, and will not affect GML compilation. These do not apply to all scripts.

data.name: GML_AlwaysUseSemicolons_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, all statements that can have a semicolon after them will.

data.name: GML_IndentationStyle_Tooltip
data.value: Style of indentation to be used when printing decompiled code.

data.name: GML_OpenBlockBraceOnSameLine
data.value: Open block braces on same line

data.name: GML_OpenBlockBraceOnSameLine_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, the curly braces used to open a block will be placed on the same line as the statement it belongs to, rather than placing the brace on its own line.

data.name: GML_RemoveSingleLineBlockBraces
data.value: Remove block braces if single line

data.name: GML_RemoveSingleLineBlockBraces_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, blocks that contain a single line will have its braces removed, where possible. For if/else chains, all blocks must be single lines.

data.name: GML_CreateEnumDeclarations_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, enum declarations identified during decompilation will always be printed to decompiled output.

data.name: GML_EnumMacroDeclarationsAtTop
data.value: Enum/macro declarations at top of code

data.name: GML_EnumMacroDeclarationsAtTop_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, enums and macros will appear at the start (top) of decompiled output, rather than at the end (bottom).

data.name: GML_PrintWarnings_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, warnings generated during decompilation will be printed as comments.

data.name: GML_AllowLeftoverDataOnStack
data.value: Allow leftover data on VM stack

data.name: GML_AllowLeftoverDataOnStack_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, data left over on the VM stack will be treated as a warning rather than an error.

data.name: GML_AddEmptyLines_Tooltip
data.value: Select specific syntax to add empty lines before, after, or around.

data.name: GML_EmptyLineAroundBranchStatements_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before and after branch statements.

data.name: GML_EmptyLineAfterBlockLocals_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line after block-scoped local variable declarations.

data.name: GML_EmptyLineBeforeSwitchCases_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before (a chain) of "case" entries in a switch statement.

data.name: GML_EmptyLineAfterSwitchCases_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line after (a chain) of "case" entries in a switch statement.

data.name: GML_EmptyLineAroundFunctionDeclarations_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before and after a function declaration, even if it is anonymous.

data.name: GML_EmptyLineAroundStaticInitialization_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before and after static initialization, grouping together consecutive statics if possible.

data.name: GML_PerformCleanups_Tooltip
data.value: Select specific cleanup operations for the decompiler to perform. Disabling these may make code more ugly or "incorrect," but can show underlying logic.

data.name: GML_CleanupTryCatchFinally_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, compiler-generated code and logic for try/catch/finally statements is rewritten to (attempt to) look like the original code.

data.name: GML_CleanupElseToContinue_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, certain usages of "else" inside of a loop can be transformed into "continue" statements, which can make code easier to read. This only happens if accuracy can be maintained.

data.name: GML_CleanupDefaultArgumentValues_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, syntax for default named function arguments will be used. Compiler-generated if statements are transformed to do this, maintaining accuracy.

data.name: GML_CleanupBuiltinArrayVariables_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, certain builtin globals (such as "view_xview"), when detected to be used without an array accessor, will be printed as such, rather than using compiler-generated syntax.

data.name: GML_CleanupLocalVarDeclarations_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, local variable declarations will be placed according to their scopes, which are detected based on usage in the code.

data.name: Editor_LanguageNote
data.value: Note: the languages currently don't seem used, and so the editor for them has not yet been made.

data.name: Editor_Sprite_ExportAllFrames
data.value: Export all frames (or Spine data if this is a Spine sprite)

data.name: Editor_BG_ClickTileHint
data.value: Hint: You can click on any tile region below to highlight its ID above.

data.name: Editor_Font_ClickGlyphHint
data.value: Hint: You can click on any glyph here to highlight it in the "Glyphs".

data.name: Editor_Font_SelectRegionEmptyGlyph
data.value: Select a region of an empty glyph

data.name: Editor_Font_GlyphSortNote
data.value: Note that the glyphs need to be specified in ascending order. Press the button below to sort them appropriately.

data.name: Editor_Font_UpdateRangeNote
data.value: Also, if you have added new characters or changed the character of an existing glyph, you should update the font range. Press the button below to do that.

data.name: Editor_Path_TodoPreview
data.value: TODO: The preview doesn't update live, I know, just close and reopen the object for now

data.name: Editor_TexPageItem_FindAllReferences
data.value: Find all references to this page item

data.name: Editor_EmbeddedTex_MouseHint
data.value: Hint: This image is mouse interactable (3 mouse buttons and mouse wheel).

data.name: Editor_Extension_ThingsToNote
data.value: THINGS TO NOTE: - Keep in mind that .gml files are either not present, or will be empty. (e.g. no functions) Because they are prebaked into GML scripts. That is completely normal.

data.name: Editor_ExtFunc_KindRunnerNote
data.value: Runner literally doesn't care about the 'Kind' value. The 'ID' does matter though, so make sure you don't have any overlaps.

data.name: Room_TileSelectHint
data.value: Hint: You can select a tile by clicking on it. To select a region of tile, hold down ALT.

data.name: Room_ContainsGameObjects
data.value: Contains GameObject instances, listed in the tree on the left

data.name: Room_ContainsLegacyTiles
data.value: Contains legacy tiles and sprites, listed in the tree on the left

data.name: Msg_WelcomeDescription
data.value: Open a data.win file to get started, then double click on the items on the left to view them.

data.name: Msg_WelcomeDescriptionLoaded
data.value: Double click on the items on the left to view them!

data.name: Msg_WelcomeDescriptionNoData
data.value: Open data.win file to get started, then double click on the items on the left to view them

data.name: Msg_NewFileDescription
data.value: New file created, have fun making a game out of nothing I TOLD YOU to open a data.win, not create a new file! :P

data.name: Msg_ExpandListToEdit
data.value: Expand the list on the left to edit items

data.name: Msg_MarkedForExportTooltip
data.value: Whether the currently open asset is marked to be exported to the currently open project, the next time it is saved.

data.name: Msg_ObjectIDTooltip
data.value: This is an ID (index) of the currently open asset, item, or object. It starts from 0.

data.name: Msg_FirstTimeSetup
data.value: First-time setup: Would you like to associate GameMaker data files (like .win) with UndertaleModTool?

data.name: Msg_DecompileWait
data.value: Decompiling, please wait... This can take a while on complex scripts.

data.name: Msg_LoadError
data.value: An error occurred while trying to load: {0}

data.name: Msg_SaveError
data.value: An error occurred while trying to save: {0}

data.name: Msg_RecompileError
data.value: An error occurred while trying to recompile code sources: {0}

data.name: Msg_CodeEditorSaveError
data.value: The changes in code editor weren't saved due to some error in "SaveCodeChanges()".

data.name: Msg_ErrorsDuringLoading
data.value: Errors occurred during loading. High chance of data loss! Proceed at your own risk.

data.name: Msg_FailedLoadSettings
data.value: Failed to load settings.json! Using default values. {0}

data.name: Msg_WarningsDuringLoading
data.value: Warnings occurred during loading. Data loss will likely occur when trying to save!

data.name: Msg_UnsupportedBytecodeVersion
data.value: Only bytecode versions 13 to 17 are supported for now, you are trying to load {0}. A lot of code is disabled and will likely break something. Saving/exporting is disabled.

data.name: Msg_YYC
data.value: This game uses YYC (YoYo Compiler), which means the code is embedded into the game executable. This configuration is currently not fully supported; continue at your own risk.

data.name: Msg_GMSDebugger
data.value: This game is set to run with the GameMaker Studio debugger and the normal runtime will simply hang after loading if the debugger is not running. You can turn this off in General Info by checking the "Disable Debugger" box and saving.

data.name: Msg_UnsupportedTextures
data.value: This game contains texture(s) with unknown or unsupported image formats. These will save/load, but will not display in editors, and many operations will fail regarding them. Proceed with caution.

data.name: Msg_SavingInDebugMode
data.value: You are saving the game in GameMaker Studio debug mode. Unless the debugger is running, the normal runtime will simply hang after loading. You can turn this off in General Info by checking the "Disable Debugger" box and saving.

data.name: Msg_UpdaterDidntExit
data.value: UndertaleModToolUpdater app didn't exit. Can't delete its temp folder.

data.name: Msg_UpdaterTempFolderError
data.value: The updater temp folder can't be deleted. Error - {0}.

data.name: Msg_FileMustBeSavedToRun
data.value: The file must be saved in order to be run.

data.name: Msg_YouMustSaveToRun
data.value: You must save your changes to run.

data.name: Msg_UseDebuggerOption
data.value: Use the "Run game using debugger" option to run this game.

data.name: Msg_NullGameExeName
data.value: Null game executable name or location

data.name: Msg_FailedFindGameExePath
data.value: Failed to find valid game executable path; escaped directory

data.name: Msg_CannotFindGameExePath
data.value: Cannot find game executable path, expected: {0}

data.name: Msg_CannotFindDataFilePath
data.value: Cannot find data file path, expected: {0}

data.name: Msg_CannotFindGamePath
data.value: Cannot find game path, expected to find it at: {0}

data.name: Msg_FailedOpenChildFile
data.value: Failed to open child data file; escaped directory.

data.name: Msg_SelectedRuntimeNoDebug
data.value: The selected runtime does not support debugging.

data.name: Msg_NotEnoughSpace
data.value: Not enough space on the system drive {0} - at least 500 MB is required.

data.name: Msg_FailedFindLatestBuild
data.value: Failed to find latest build! Detected action name - {0}

data.name: Msg_AlreadyUpToDate
data.value: UndertaleModTool is already up to date. Update anyway?

data.name: Msg_32BitOn64Bit
data.value: Detected 32-bit (x86) version of UndertaleModTool on an 64-bit operating system.

data.name: Msg_UpdaterNotFound
data.value: Updater not found! Aborting update, report this to the devs! Location checked: {0}

data.name: Msg_CantCopyUpdater
data.value: Can't copy the updater app to the temporary folder. {0}

data.name: Msg_FailedDownloadNewVersion
data.value: Failed to download new version of UndertaleModTool. Error - {0}.

data.name: Msg_CantCopyItemName
data.value: Can't copy the item name to clipboard due to this error: {0}

data.name: Msg_ErrorInObjectReferencesWindow
data.value: An error occurred in the object references related window.

data.name: Msg_ErrorAddingMenuItem
Msg_CantFindCodeEntry
data.value: Can't find code entry "{0}". (probably, different game data was loaded)

data.name: Msg_StoppingProgressBarFailed
data.value: Stopping the progress bar updater task is failed. It's highly recommended to restart the application.

data.name: Msg_ProjectOpenMakeNew
data.value: Warning: you currently have a project open. Are you sure you want to make a new project?

data.name: Msg_SaveToProjectDataFile
data.value: Save to the project's designated data file for saving?

data.name: Msg_ProjectOpenOpenAnother
data.value: A project is currently open - open another data file and discard all unsaved changes?

data.name: Msg_AssetsMarkedForExportQuit
data.value: There are assets marked to be exported in the current project. Really quit?

data.name: Msg_ScriptStillRunSaveAnyway
data.value: Script still runs. Save anyway? It can corrupt the data file that you'll save.

data.name: Msg_DeleteObjectShiftNote
data.value:
Delete {0}?
Note that the code often references objects by ID, so this operation is likely to break stuff because other items will shift up!

data.name: Msg_InvalidIdentifierAddAutoName
data.value: Asset name "{0}" is not a valid identifier. Add a new asset using an auto-generated name instead?

data.name: Msg_AddToRoomOrderList
data.value: Add the new room to the end of the room order list?

data.name: Msg_DebuggerEnabledDisableToRun
data.value: The game has the debugger enabled. Would you like to disable it so the game will run?

data.name: Msg_RunGMSDebuggerConfirm
data.value: Are you sure that you want to run the game with GMS debugger?

data.name: Cmd_ToggleBrushTiling
data.value: Toggle the "tiling" behavior on multi-tile brushes

data.name: DebugData_Question
data.value: The debugger is enabled. Do you want to generate debugger data?

data.name: DebugData_DecompileDesc
data.value: Decompile whole source code when saving for display in the debugger

data.name: DebugData_NoBreakpointNote
data.value: NOTE: no breakpoint support yet! Except for the script entry point

data.name: DebugData_DisassemblyPartialDesc
data.value: Full disassembly, but you can set breakpoints only on B, BR, BT, POP, POPZ, RET and EXIT. This is still a lot more than that poor GM:S debugger was designed to handle, so allow up to 5 minutes for it to load the data file (yes, seriously)

data.name: DebugData_DisassemblyFullDesc
data.value: Gave up after like 15 minutes of waiting, don't even try this

data.name: StringUpdate_Question
data.value: You edited a string reference! What do you want to do?

data.name: StringUpdate_ChangeAllOccurrences
data.value: Change all occurrences of this value

data.name: RuntimePicker_SelectRuntime
data.value: Select the runtime you want to use

data.name: ProjectAssets_Description1
data.value: Below is a list of all assets currently waiting to be exported to disk for the current project, once it is next saved.

data.name: ProjectAssets_Description2
data.value: To quickly add or remove assets from being part of the project, you can drag and drop assets here, or delete them.

data.name: FindRef_ResultsFor
data.value: The search results for the game object "".

data.name: FindRef_NoteMayNotBeComplete
data.value: Note: the results may be not 100% complete. Use one of the search scripts in the "Find" menu if necessary.

data.name: Common_ObjectRefToolTip
data.value: This is an object reference. Drag and drop an object of matching type from the tree on the left to change it!

data.name: Msg_LoadErrorDetail
data.value: An error occurred while trying to load: {0}

data.name: Msg_SaveErrorDetail
data.value: An error occurred while trying to save: {0}

data.name: Msg_RecompileErrorDetail
data.value: An error occurred while trying to recompile code sources: {0}

data.name: Msg_SpineSpriteWarning
data.value: This seems to be a Spine sprite, .json and .atlas files will be exported together with the frames. PLEASE EDIT THEM CAREFULLY! SOME MANUAL EDITING OF THE JSON MAY BE REQUIRED! THE DATA IS EXPORTED AS-IS.

data.name: Msg_TexturePageDimensionsNotPowerOf2
data.value: WARNING: Texture page dimensions are not powers of 2. Sprite blurring is very likely in-game.

data.name: Msg_TextureConvertedToPNG
data.value: {0} was converted into PNG format since we don't support converting images into DDS format. This might have performance issues in the game.

data.name: Msg_FailedToPlayAudioNotWavOgg
data.value: Failed to play audio! Not a WAV or OGG.

data.name: Msg_ImportNonAudioWarning
data.value:
Warning: File being imported is not a WAV or OGG. Import anyway?
This may corrupt the sound.

data.name: Msg_ImportFormatMismatchWarning
data.value:
Warning: Filetype being imported does not match existing filetype. Import anyway?
This may corrupt the sound, unless sound asset compression settings are adjusted as well.

data.name: Msg_EnableGMSDebugger
data.value: Are you sure that you want to enable GMS debugger? If you want to enable a debug mode in some game, then you need to use one of the scripts.

data.name: Msg_CodeEntryReference
data.value: This code entry is a reference to an anonymous function within {0}, view it there.

data.name: Msg_DuplicateKeyInTextdata
data.value: There is a duplicate key in textdata_en, being {0}. This may cause errors in the comment display of text.

data.name: Msg_UnknownErrorInTextdata
data.value: Unknown error in textdata_en. This may cause errors in the comment display of text.

data.name: Msg_DecompilingPleaseWait
data.value: Decompiling, please wait... This can take a while on complex scripts.

data.name: Msg_EditingDisassemblyProjectWarning
data.value:
Editing disassembly while in an open project (even through scripts) can cause desyncs with source code in the project.
The source code will not change unless you directly modify it, or if you remove the code asset from the project entirely.

data.name: Msg_CannotFindGlyphsScrollViewer
data.value: Cannot find the glyphs table scroll viewer.

data.name: Msg_GlyphRectEditorError
Msg_LastGlyphZeroSize
data.value: The last glyph has zero size. You can use the button on the left to fix that.

data.name: Msg_CharCodeExceedsMax
data.value: The character code is greater than the maximum (65535)

data.name: Msg_ValueIsNotString
data.value: The value is not a string.

data.name: Msg_FindAllTileReferences
data.value: Find all references of this tile

data.name: Msg_ReferencesWindowError
Msg_CannotFindTileIdListScrollViewer
data.value: Cannot find the tile ID list scroll viewer.

data.name: Msg_FindAllPageItemReferences
data.value: Find all references to this page item

data.name: Msg_EmptyInstancesListWarning
data.value: The instances list of layer "{0}" is empty, but the layer has the instances ID.

data.name: Msg_MaxLayerDepthReached
data.value: Warning - the maximum layer depth is reached. You probably should change the depth of the new layer.

data.name: Msg_LayersDontSupportFeature
data.value: Layers don't support this feature currently, change the layer depths instead.

data.name: Msg_CantChangeObjectPosition
data.value: Can't change the object position - no list for the selected object was found.

data.name: Msg_TileDataSizeCantBeZero
data.value: Tile data size can't be zero.

data.name: Msg_FileLineCountMismatch
data.value: Error - selected file line count doesn't match tile layer height.

data.name: Msg_LineLengthNotEqualTileWidth
data.value: Length of line {0} is not equal to the tile data width.

data.name: Msg_LayerMustHaveTileset
data.value: The layer must have a tileset set!

data.name: Msg_LayerSizeMustBeLargerBoth
data.value: The layer's horizontal and vertical size must be larger than 0 tiles! (Use the Auto button to set the tilemap size based on the room size.)

data.name: Msg_LayerHorizontalSizeMustBeLarger
data.value: The layer's horizontal size must be larger than 0 tiles! (Use the Auto button to set the tilemap size based on the room size.)

data.name: Msg_LayerVerticalSizeMustBeLarger
data.value: The layer's vertical size must be larger than 0 tiles! (Use the Auto button to set the tilemap size based on the room size.)

data.name: Msg_RoomFlagsMustContainIsGMS2
data.value: Room flags of GMS 2+ games must contain the "IsGMS2" flag, otherwise the game will crash when loading that room.

data.name: Msg_RoomFlagsMustContainGM2024_13
data.value: Room flags of GM 2024.13+ games must contain the "IsGM2024_13" flag, otherwise the game will crash when loading that room.

data.name: Msg_ErrorGeneratingTileRectangles
data.value:
An error occurred while generating "Rectangles" for tile layer {0}.
{1}

data.name: Msg_ScriptErrorWithType
data.value:
An error of type "{0}" occurred. The error is:
{1}

data.name: Msg_TempRunWarning
data.value: WARNING: Temp running the game does not permanently save your changes. Please "Save" the game to save your changes. Closing UndertaleModTool without using the "Save" option can result in loss of work.

data.name: Msg_NoDataFileLoaded
data.value: No data file is currently loaded!

data.name: Msg_CantHighlightNull
data.value: Can't highlight the object - it's null or isn't an UndertaleResource.

data.name: Msg_CantHighlightNotFound
data.value: Can't highlight the object "{0}" - element with object list not found.

data.name: Msg_WillCloseToUpdate
data.value: UndertaleModTool will now close to finish the update.

data.name: Msg_EmptyDirectoryWarning
data.value: Currently, the destination data file's directory is empty. You will likely want to copy all other game files to the destination directory, so that external assets can be loaded correctly (both in-game and in this tool), and so the game can be started.

data.name: Msg_SameDirectoryWarning
data.value: The destination data file is in the same directory as the source data file. This may permanently overwrite external data files. Proceed?

data.name: Msg_CodeRefDecompile
data.value: // This code entry is a reference to an anonymous function within "{0}", decompile that instead.

data.name: Msg_CodeRefDisassemble
data.value: ; This code entry is a reference to an anonymous function within "{0}", disassemble that instead.

data.name: Msg_FailedFindGameExe
data.value: Cannot find game executable path, expected: {0}

data.name: Msg_FailedFindDataFile
data.value: Cannot find data file path, expected: {0}

data.name: Msg_FailedFindGamePath
data.value: Cannot find game path, expected to find it at: {0}

data.name: Msg_DebuggerEnabledRun
data.value: The game has the debugger enabled. Would you like to disable it so the game will run?

data.name: Msg_MustSaveToRun
data.value: You must save your changes to run.

data.name: Msg_UseDebuggerRunOption
data.value: Use the "Run game using debugger" option to run this game.

data.name: Msg_RuntimeNoDebug
data.value: The selected runtime does not support debugging.

data.name: Msg_UpdaterAppWontExit
data.value: UndertaleModToolUpdater app didn't exit. Can't delete its temp folder.

data.name: Msg_NewProjectConfirm
data.value: Warning: you currently have a project open. Are you sure you want to make a new project?

data.name: Msg_InvalidAssetName
data.value: Asset name "{0}" is not a valid identifier. Add a new asset using an auto-generated name instead?

data.name: Msg_DeleteShiftWarning
data.value: Note that the code often references objects by ID, so this operation is likely to break stuff because other items will shift up!

data.name: Msg_ScriptStillRunSave
data.value: Script still runs. Save anyway? It can corrupt the data file that you'll save.

data.name: Msg_UnexportedAssetsQuit
data.value: There are assets marked to be exported in the current project. Really quit?

data.name: Msg_UnexportedAssetsCloseProject
data.value: There are assets marked to be exported in the current project. Really close?

data.name: Msg_ProjectOpenDiscardChanges
data.value: A project is currently open - open another data file and discard all unsaved changes?

data.name: Msg_NewProjectDiscardUnexported
data.value: There are assets marked to be exported in the current project - create a new project and discard all unexported changes?

data.name: Msg_OpenProjectDiscardUnexported
data.value: There are assets marked to be exported in the current project - open another new project and discard all unexported changes?

data.name: Msg_ClipboardCopyError
data.value: Can't copy the item name to clipboard due to this error: {0}. You probably should try again.

data.name: Msg_AddMenuItemError
Msg_CodeEntryNotFound
data.value: Can't find code entry "{0}". (probably, different game data was loaded)

data.name: Msg_FailedFindBuild
data.value: Failed to find latest build! Detected action name - {0}

data.name: Msg_FailedToDownload
data.value: Failed to download new version of UndertaleModTool. Error - {0}.

data.name: Msg_NullGameExe
data.value: Null game executable name or location

data.name: Msg_CodeEditorSaveFailed
data.value: The changes in code editor weren't saved due to some error in "SaveCodeChanges()".

data.name: Msg_BytecodeVersionUnsupported
data.value: Only bytecode versions 13 to 17 are supported for now, you are trying to load {0}. A lot of code is disabled and will likely break something. Saving/exporting is disabled.

data.name: Msg_YYCGame
data.value: This game uses YYC (YoYo Compiler), which means the code is embedded into the game executable. This configuration is currently not fully supported; continue at your own risk.

data.name: Msg_GMSDebuggerEnabled
data.value: This game is set to run with the GameMaker Studio debugger and the normal runtime will simply hang after loading if the debugger is not running. You can turn this off in General Info by checking the "Disable Debugger" box and saving.

data.name: Msg_SavingGMSDebugMode
data.value: You are saving the game in GameMaker Studio debug mode. Unless the debugger is running, the normal runtime will simply hang after loading. You can turn this off in General Info by checking the "Disable Debugger" box and saving.

data.name: Msg_ErrorCreatingProject
data.value: Error occurred when creating new project: {0}

data.name: Msg_CannotDetermineGMVersion
data.value: Cannot determine GameMaker version - "General Info" is null.

data.name: Msg_CannotGetSourceTypes
data.value: Cannot get the source types for object of type "{0}".

data.name: Msg_AtLeastOneTypeSelected
data.value: At least one type should be selected.

data.name: Msg_StringsSearchWarning
data.value: You have selected the "Strings" when there are a lot of strings. That could make the search process noticeably longer. Do you want to proceed?

data.name: Msg_FailedFindAudioGroupFile
data.value: Failed to find audio group file.

data.name: Msg_FailedPlayAudioNoOptions
data.value: Failed to play audio! No options for playback worked.

data.name: Msg_CodeEntryRoomExists
data.value: Code entry for room already exists; reusing it.

data.name: Msg_AddingNotSupported
data.value: Adding not supported in this situation.

data.name: Msg_NotUndertaleResource
data.value: The selected object is not an "UndertaleResource".

data.name: Msg_TilesetWrongParams
data.value: Tileset of "{0}" tile layer has wrong parameters (tile size, output border, etc.). It can't be displayed.

data.name: Msg_ErrorRenderingTileLayer
data.value:
An error occurred while rendering tile layer "{0}".
{1}

data.name: Msg_ObjectTypeNoEditor
data.value: The type of this object doesn't have an editor/viewer.

data.name: Msg_UnableToFindRuntime
data.value: Unable to find game EXE or any installed Studio runtime

data.name: Setup_FileAssociationQuestion
data.value: First-time setup: Would you like to associate GameMaker data files (like .win) with UndertaleModTool?

data.name: Settings_RemoveFileAssociations
data.value: Remove current file associations, if they exist?

data.name: Settings_DarkModeMsgBoxWarning
data.value: The message boxes (like this one) aren't compatible with the dark mode. This will be fixed in future versions.

data.name: Ctrl_StringRefToolTip
data.value: This is a string reference. Change the value here to edit this just this value of all referenced values, or drag and drop another string instance from the tree on the left to change the reference

data.name: Main_WelcomeDescription
data.value: Open a data.win file to get started, then double click on the items on the left to view them.

data.name: Main_WelcomeDescription2
data.value: New file created, have fun making a game out of nothing I TOLD YOU to open a data.win, not create a new file! :P

data.name: Main_DoubleClickToView
data.value: Double click on the items on the left to view them

data.name: Main_DoubleClickToView2
data.value: Double click on the items on the left to view them!

data.name: Main_OpenDataWinToStart
data.value: Open data.win file to get started

data.name: Main_MarkedForExportToolTip
data.value: Whether the currently open asset is marked to be exported to the currently open project, the next time it is saved.

data.name: Main_ObjectIdToolTip
data.value: This is an ID (index) of the currently open asset, item, or object. It starts from 0.

data.name: Editor_SizeHint
data.value: Hint: Prior to GM 2023.8, these "Size" values were not split by x and y. UndertaleModTool represents this by averaging the values. For best results, enter the same value in both fields.

data.name: Editor_ExtFunc_KindNote
data.value: Runner literally doesn't care about the 'Kind' value. The 'ID' does matter though, so make sure you don't have any overlaps.

data.name: Tile_MirrorX
data.value: Mirror the brush around the X axis (X)

data.name: Tile_FlipY
data.value: Flip the brush around the Y axis (Y/Z)

data.name: Tile_ShowRoomBehind
data.value: Show the rest of the room behind the tilemap (P)

data.name: Tile_EnableTiling
data.value: Enable the "tiling" behavior when using multi-tile brushes (B)

data.name: Tile_TilesetColumnsToolTip
data.value: Number of columns to use for the tile palette. Adjust if the tileset looks broken. Persists when the tile editor is closed, but not when the file is saved and reopened.

data.name: Tile_ControlsHint
data.value: Ctrl+LMB: Fill (+Shift: Global) Alt+LMB: Pick MMB: Scroll/Pick

data.name: Ctrl_ObjRefToolTip
data.value: This is an object reference. Drag and drop an object of matching type from the tree on the left to change it!

data.name: RuntimePicker_UnableToFindRuntime
data.value: Unable to find game EXE or any installed Studio runtime

data.name: ProjectAssets_NoEditorForType
data.value: The type of this object doesn't have an editor/viewer.

data.name: Settings_EnableAssetOrderSwapping_Tooltip
data.value: Toggles dragging & dropping assets in the asset tabs to different positions in the list. Disabled by default.

data.name: Settings_WarnTempRun_Tooltip
data.value: Warn about temp running. Enabled by default.

data.name: Settings_RecompileAllOnProjectSave
data.value: Recompile all source GML when saving a project data file

data.name: Settings_RecompileAllOnProjectSave_Tooltip
data.value: Whether to recompile all GML source code included in the currently-loaded project, if one is loaded, whenever saving a data file (including temp runs). Disabled by default.

data.name: GML_UseCSSHexColorLiterals_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, color constants can be formatted in the #RRGGBB hex format.

data.name: GML_NamedInstanceIDPrefix_Tooltip
data.value: Prefix used for named instance IDs when found by decompiler, and for compilation. Change this value if it conflicts with variable names.

data.name: GML_OpenBlockBracesOnSameLine
data.value: Open block braces on same line

data.name: GML_OpenBlockBracesOnSameLine_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, the curly braces used to open a block will be placed on the same line as the statement it belongs to, rather than placing the brace on its own line.

data.name: GML_RemoveBlockBracesIfSingleLine
data.value: Remove block braces if single line

data.name: GML_RemoveBlockBracesIfSingleLine_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, blocks that contain a single line will have its braces removed, where possible. For if/else chains, all blocks must be single lines.

data.name: GML_AroundBranchStatements_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before and after branch statements.

data.name: GML_AfterLocalVarDeclarations_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line after block-scoped local variable declarations.

data.name: GML_BeforeSwitchCases_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before (a chain) of "case" entries in a switch statement.

data.name: GML_AfterSwitchCases_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line after (a chain) of "case" entries in a switch statement.

data.name: GML_AroundFunctionDeclarations_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before and after a function declaration, even if it is anonymous.

data.name: GML_AroundStaticInitialization_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, adds an empty line before and after static initialization, grouping together consecutive statics if possible.

data.name: GML_TryCatchFinallyRewriting_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, compiler-generated code and logic for try/catch/finally statements is rewritten to (attempt to) look like the original code.

data.name: GML_TransformElseToContinue_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, certain usages of "else" inside of a loop can be transformed into "continue" statements, which can make code easier to read. This only happens if accuracy can be maintained.

data.name: GML_DefaultArgumentValues_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, syntax for default named function arguments will be used. Compiler-generated if statements are transformed to do this, maintaining accuracy.

data.name: GML_BuiltinArrayVariableSyntax_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, certain builtin globals (such as "view_xview"), when detected to be used without an array accessor, will be printed as such, rather than using compiler-generated syntax.

data.name: GML_ScopeLocalVarDeclarations_Tooltip
data.value: If enabled, local variable declarations will be placed according to their scopes, which are detected based on usage in the code.

data.name: Editor_ExportAllFrames
data.value: Export all frames (or Spine data if this is a Spine sprite)

data.name: Editor_FontHint
data.value: Hint: You can click on any glyph here to highlight it in the "Glyphs".

data.name: Editor_SelectRegionOfEmptyGlyph
data.value: Select a region of an empty glyph

data.name: Editor_SortGlyphsNote
data.value: Note that the glyphs need to be specified in ascending order. Press the button below to sort them appropriately.

data.name: Editor_UpdateRangeNote
data.value: Also, if you have added new characters or changed the character of an existing glyph, you should update the font range. Press the button below to do that.

data.name: Editor_TileHint
data.value: Hint: You can click on any tile region below to highlight its ID above.

data.name: Room_TileSelectionHint
data.value: Hint: You can select a tile by clicking on it. To select a region of tile, hold down ALT.

data.name: Msg_FileAssociationsQuestion
data.value: Do you want to associate UndertaleModTool with GameMaker data files?

data.name: Ctrl_MarkedForExport_Tooltip
data.value: Whether the currently open asset is marked to be exported to the currently open project, the next time it is saved.

data.name: Ctrl_ObjectID_Tooltip
data.value: This is an ID (index) of the currently open asset, item, or object. It starts from 0.

data.name: StringUpdate_Message
data.value: You edited a string reference! What do you want to do?

data.name: DebugData_Message
data.value: The debugger is enabled. Do you want to generate debugger data?

data.name: DebugData_DecompiledDescription
data.value: Decompile whole source code when saving for display in the debugger

data.name: DebugData_NoteNoBreakpoints
data.value: NOTE: no breakpoint support yet! Except for the script entry point

data.name: DebugData_DisassemblyPartialDescription
data.value: Full disassembly, but you can set breakpoints only on B, BR, BT, POP, POPZ, RET and EXIT. This is still a lot more than that poor GM:S debugger was designed to handle, so allow up to 5 minutes for it to load the data file (yes, seriously)

data.name: DebugData_DisassemblyFullDescription
data.value: Gave up after like 15 minutes of waiting, don't even try this

data.name: FindRef_SearchResultsFor
data.value: The search results for the game object "".

data.name: Editor_ClickTileRegionHint
data.value: Hint: You can click on any tile region below to select it and edit its properties.

data.name: Editor_ExportAllFramesOrSpine
data.value: Export all frames (or Spine data if this is a Spine sprite)

data.name: Editor_GlyphHint
data.value: Hint: You can click on any glyph rectangle below to select it and edit its properties, or double-click to add a new glyph.

data.name: Editor_OffsetTooltip
data.value: The number of pixels to horizontally offset the rendering of the glyph. It's highlighed in blue on the texture above.

data.name: Editor_UpdateRangeNote2
data.value: Updates the range of the currently displayed glyphs to match the font texture.

data.name: Msg_32BitOSWarning
data.value: Your operating system is 32-bit. The 32-bit (x86) version of UndertaleModTool is obsolete.

data.name: Msg_CantCopyToClipboard
data.value: Can't copy the item name to clipboard due to this error: {0}

data.name: Msg_CantFindDataFile
data.value: Cannot find data file path, expected: {0}

data.name: Msg_CantFindGameExe
data.value: Cannot find game executable path, expected: {0}

data.name: Msg_CantFindGameExeAt
data.value: Cannot find game executable path, expected to find it at: {0}

data.name: Msg_CantFindGamePath
data.value: Cannot find game path, expected to find it at: {0}

data.name: Msg_CantHighlightListNotFound
data.value: Can't highlight the object "{0}" - element with object list not found.

data.name: Msg_FailedPlayAudioNoPlayback
data.value: Failed to play audio! No options for playback worked.

data.name: Msg_GameExePathEscaped
data.value: Failed to find valid game executable path; escaped directory

data.name: Msg_GeneralInfoNull
data.value: Cannot determine GameMaker version - "General Info" is null.

data.name: Msg_InvalidNameRegex
data.value: The search term is not a valid regex pattern.

data.name: Msg_LayerInstancesEmpty
data.value: The instances list of layer "{0}" is empty, but the layer has the instances ID.

data.name: Msg_LoadingWarningsDataLoss
data.value: Warnings occurred during loading. Data loss will likely occur when trying to save!

data.name: Msg_ManyStringsSelectedWarning
data.value: You have selected the "Strings" when there are a lot of strings. That could make the search process noticeably longer. Do you want to proceed?

data.name: Msg_OneCodeEntryError
data.value: One code entry matched the filter.

data.name: Msg_PathNotExist
data.value: Path {0} does not exist, cannot search for files!

data.name: Msg_ProjectCurrentlyOpen
data.value: A project is currently open - open another data file and discard all unsaved changes?

data.name: Msg_ProjectOpenNewConfirm
data.value: Warning: you currently have a project open. Are you sure you want to make a new project?

data.name: Msg_ProjectOpenOpenDataFile
data.value: A project is currently open - open another data file and discard all unsaved changes?

data.name: Msg_ScriptStillRunsSave
data.value: Script still runs. Save anyway? It can corrupt the data file that you'll save.

data.name: Msg_TilesetWrongParameters
data.value: Tileset of "{0}" tile layer has wrong parameters (tile size, output border, etc.). It can't be displayed.

data.name: Msg_UnexportedAssetsOpenProject
data.value: There are assets marked to be exported in the current project - open another new project and discard all unexported changes?

data.name: Msg_UnsupportedTexturesWarning
data.value: This game contains texture(s) with unknown or unsupported image formats. These will save/load, but will not display in editors, and many operations will fail regarding them. Proceed with caution.

data.name: Msg_YYCWarning
data.value: This game uses YYC (YoYo Compiler), which means the code is embedded into the game executable. This configuration is currently not fully supported; continue at your own risk.

data.name: Room_TileHint1
data.value: Hint: You can click on any tile region below to select it and edit its properties.

data.name: Settings_AssetOrderSwappingToolTip
data.value: Whether to allow changing the order of assets by dragging and dropping them in the tree view.

data.name: Settings_GMS2PathToolTip
data.value: The path to the GameMaker Studio 2 runtimes directory.

data.name: Settings_RecompileOnSaveToolTip
data.value: Whether to recompile all GML code sources when saving a project data file.

data.name: Editor_ExtensionNote1
data.value: - Keep in mind that .gml files are either not present, or will be empty. (e.g. no functions)

data.name: Editor_ExtensionNote2
data.value: Because they are prebaked into GML scripts. That is completely normal.

data.name: Editor_GlyphSortNote1
data.value: Note that the glyphs need to be specified in ascending order.

data.name: Editor_PathPreviewNote
data.value: TODO: The preview doesn't update live, but it will after switching to another tab and back.

data.name: Editor_SelectRegionEmptyGlyph
data.value: Select a region of an empty glyph

data.name: Editor_ShiftTooltip
data.value: The number of pixels to shift right when advancing to the next character. It's highlighed in green on the texture above.

data.name: Editor_TextureMouseInteractableHint
data.value: Hint: This image is mouse interactable. You can click on it to select the corresponding texture page item.

data.name: Editor_UpdateRangeNote3
data.value: Note: the glyphs need to be specified in ascending order for this to work correctly.

data.name: Main_ExpandListToEdit
data.value: Expand the list on the left to edit items

data.name: Main_NewFileDescription
data.value: New file created, have fun making a game out of nothing I TOLD YOU to open a data.win, not create a new file! :P

data.name: Menu_FindAllReferencesOfTile
data.value: Find all references of this tile

data.name: Menu_FindAllReferencesToPageItem
data.value: Find all references to this page item

data.name: Msg_32BitProcessOn64BitOS
data.value: Detected 32-bit (x86) version of UndertaleModTool on an 64-bit operating system. It's highly recommended to use the 64-bit version instead. Do you wish to download it?

data.name: Msg_CantOpenResultsWhileInProgress
data.value: Can't open the results while the search is still in progress.

data.name: Msg_CantSearchWhileInProgress
data.value: Can't start a new search while one is still in progress.

data.name: Msg_CantSearchYYC
data.value: Cannot search in YYC compiled games.

data.name: Msg_ChildFileEscaped
data.value: Failed to open child data file; escaped directory.

data.name: Msg_ChooseNewProjectName
data.value: Choose a name for the new project

data.name: Msg_DeleteItemConfirmWithWarning
data.value: Delete {0}? Note that the code often references objects by ID, so this operation is likely to break stuff because other items will shift up!

data.name: Msg_DisableDebuggerQuestion
data.value: The game has the debugger enabled. Would you like to disable it so the game will run?

data.name: Msg_FailedToChooseOutputFileName
data.value: Failed to choose the output file name.

data.name: Msg_FailedToFindBuild
data.value: Failed to find latest build! Detected action name - {0}

data.name: Msg_GMSDebuggerSaveWarning
data.value: You are saving the game in GameMaker Studio debug mode. Unless the debugger is running, the normal runtime will simply hang after loading. You can turn this off in General Info by checking the "Disable Debugger" box and saving.

data.name: Msg_GMSDebuggerWarning
data.value: This game is set to run with the GameMaker Studio debugger and the normal runtime will simply hang after loading if the debugger is not running. You can turn this off in General Info by checking the "Disable Debugger" box and saving.

data.name: Msg_MultipleCodeEntriesError
data.value: Multiple code entries matched the filter.

data.name: Msg_NoCodeEntriesMatchFilter
data.value: No code entries matched the filter.

data.name: Msg_NoDataWinLoaded
data.value: No data file is currently loaded!

data.name: Msg_ObjectReferenceIsStale
data.value: The reference of game object "{0}" is stale (the referenced object was deleted).

data.name: Msg_ObjectReferenceNoEditor
data.value: The type of this object doesn't have an editor/viewer.

data.name: Msg_ObjectReferencesAreStale
data.value: The references of game object "{0}" are stale (some referenced objects were deleted).

data.name: Msg_ObjectReferencesWindowError
Msg_ProgressBarUpdaterRunning
data.value: Stopping the progress bar updater task is failed. It's highly recommended to restart the application.

data.name: Msg_RuntimeNoDebugging
data.value: The selected runtime does not support debugging.

data.name: Msg_RunWithDebuggerQuestion
data.value: Are you sure that you want to run the game with GMS debugger? If you want to enable a debug mode in some game, then you need to use one of the scripts.

data.name: Msg_SelectedObjectNotUndertaleResource
data.value: The selected object is not an "UndertaleResource".

data.name: Msg_StopProgressBarFailed
data.value: Stopping the progress bar updater task is failed. It's highly recommended to restart the application.

data.name: Msg_TabContentStateRestoreFail
data.value: The last tab content state can't be restored - "UserControl" is not found.

data.name: Msg_TabContentStateRestoreFailScrollViewer
data.value: The last tab content state can't be restored - "ScrollViewer" is not found.

data.name: Msg_TabContentStateSaveFail
data.value: The last tab content state can't be saved - "UserControl" is not found.

data.name: Msg_TooManyCodeLines
data.value: Too many code lines to display.

data.name: Msg_UnexportedAssetsNewProject
data.value: There are assets marked to be exported in the current project - create a new project and discard all unexported changes?

data.name: Room_AutoSizeTooltip
data.value: Automatically set the tilemap size based on the room size

data.name: Settings_AutoFileAssociationToolTip
data.value: Whether to automatically associate GameMaker data file types with UndertaleModTool.

data.name: Settings_EnableDarkModeToolTip
data.value: Whether to enable the dark mode theme.

data.name: Settings_GMS1PathToolTip
data.value: The path to the GameMaker: Studio 1.4 installation directory.

data.name: Settings_GridHeightToolTip
data.value: Whether to override the global grid height for the room editor.

data.name: Settings_GridThicknessToolTip
data.value: Whether to override the global grid thickness for the room editor.

data.name: Settings_GridWidthToolTip
data.value: Whether to override the global grid width for the room editor.

data.name: Settings_LanguageToolTip
data.value: Select the display language for the application.

data.name: Settings_RecompileOnSave
data.value: Recompile all source GML when saving a project data file

data.name: Settings_RememberWindowPlacementsToolTip
data.value: Whether to remember the last window position and size.

data.name: Settings_ShowDebuggerOptionToolTip
data.value: Whether to show the "Run game under GMS debugger" option in the File menu.

data.name: Settings_ShowNullEntriesToolTip
data.value: Whether to show null entries in the resource tree view.

data.name: Settings_WarnOnCloseToolTip
data.value: Whether to show a warning when closing with unsaved changes.

data.name: Settings_WarnTempRunToolTip
data.value: Whether to show a warning when temporarily running the game.

data.name: Settings_Language_Note1
data.value: The language of open tabs will only take effect after restarting the application.
